title: 10 best herbal essential oils for itchy scalp
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for itchy scalp are natural remedies that help alleviate discomfort and irritation on the scalp. These oils are derived from plants and have antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the scalp. The best herbal essential oils for itchy scalp include Melaleuca alternifolia, Rosmarinus officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Cymbopogon citratus, Aloe barbadensis, Eucalyptus globulus, Cinnamomum camphora, Thymus vulgaris, Santalum album, and Sesamum indicum. If left untreated, itchy scalp can lead to anxiety, depression, and even social isolation, negatively impacting one's self-esteem and overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with itchy scalp because of its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and soothing properties. The oil's natural antimicrobial agents help to reduce inflammation and irritation on the scalp, providing relief from itchy and flaky skin. Additionally, lavender's calming effects can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, common triggers for itchy scalps. When massaged into the scalp, English lavender essential oils create a calming and refreshing environment, allowing the skin to heal and soothe naturally.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: English lavender essential oil has antiseptic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth on the scalp, reducing itchiness and flaking.
Anti-inflammatory: Lavender oil's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe irritated skin and reduce redness, providing relief from itching and discomfort associated with an itchy scalp.
Calming: The calming effects of lavender oil can help to relax and calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that may contribute to itchiness on the scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lavender roots help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and irritation.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a high concentration of volatile oils that target skin issues like itching and inflammation, making them effective against itchy scalps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavender flowers lavendula angustifolia in the morning after dew has evaporated and dry them completely.
Combine 100g of dried lavender flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or jojoba oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14-21 days shaking the jar every day to facilitate infusion and allow the essential oils to release from the flowers.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserve the infused oil.

Allow the infused oil to stand at room temperature for 24 hours before bottling it in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids for up to 6 months use as an herbal essential oil treatment for itchy scalp.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with itchy scalp because of its natural antifungal and antibacterial properties, which effectively combat dandruff, flakiness, and itchiness caused by fungal or bacterial infections. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also soothe irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate excess sebum and dead skin cells, promoting a healthy scalp environment that allows for optimal hair growth. This natural remedy can provide relief from itchy scalp conditions, leaving your scalp feeling calm, cool, and collected.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: TTO's antifungal activity helps to combat fungal infections such as ringworm, which can cause itching and irritation on the scalp.
Anti-inflammatory properties: TTO's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and redness caused by irritants or allergic reactions, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Antimicrobial properties: TTO's antimicrobial activity helps to kill bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to an itchy scalp, such as staph infections or dandruff.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antifungal and antibacterial properties, tea tree leaves are often used to create essential oils that can help soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, tea tree roots are valued for their ability to reduce redness and irritation associated with an itchy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality melaleuca alternifolia leaves in sufficient quantity for the desired oil yield.
Dry the leaves thoroughly by spreading them out in a single layer and allowing them to air dry for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a glass still or a commercial essential oil distiller with water at a ratio of 1:5 for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towels to remove impurities and sediment.
Bottle the final product in dark glass containers and store it in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months, shaking well before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with itchy scalp because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. It has been shown to reduce dandruff, itching, and flaking caused by fungal and bacterial infections. The oil's antioxidant compounds also help soothe irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, rosemary oil stimulates blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and improving circulation, which can help alleviate itchy feelings and leave the scalp feeling calm and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary essential oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation on the scalp, which can contribute to itchiness.

Anti-fungal and antimicrobial properties: The antifungal and antimicrobial properties of rosemary essential oil help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchy scalps by preventing their growth and spread.

Stimulating and improving blood circulation properties: Rosemary essential oil's ability to stimulate and improve blood circulation helps increase oxygenation and nutrient supply to the scalp, which can alleviate itchiness and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of rosemary contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
Roots: Rosemary roots have been used traditionally in aromatherapy to reduce dandruff and other scalp irritations, due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves from your garden or purchase high-quality dried herbs in bulk.
Dry the harvested leaves for at least 24 hours to reduce moisture content using air circulation or a dehydrator.
Blend 50 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 200 ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the herbal mixture in a glass jar for 2 weeks, shaking daily, allowing the oils to infuse and extract beneficial compounds.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and preserving the aromatic essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with itchy scalp because of its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The oil's ability to soothe and calm irritated skin helps to reduce itching and flaking associated with conditions such as dandruff, psoriasis, and eczema. Sage oil also has natural antifungal and antibacterial qualities that can help control the growth of yeast and bacteria on the scalp, which can contribute to itchy and flaky patches. As a result, sage essential oils can provide relief from an itchy scalp and promote healthy, balanced skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage essential oil's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress on the scalp, which can contribute to itchiness and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness on the scalp, providing relief from itching and discomfort caused by conditions like dandruff or psoriasis.

Antifungal and antibacterial properties: Sage essential oil's antifungal and antibacterial properties help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness, irritation, and flaking on the scalp.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are a popular choice for making essential oils due to their potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated scalps.

Barks: The barks of the sage plant contain flavonoids and terpenes that have antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating itchy scalp conditions caused by fungal or bacterial infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves from the garden or store-bought sources weighing approximately one ounce.
Cleanse the harvested leaves by gently rinsing them with warm water and pat drying with a paper towel to remove impurities.
Combine the cleansed leaves with one cup of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar for maceration overnight at room temperature.
After eight hours, strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids and retain the herbal-infused carrier oil.

Transfer the infused oil to a double boiler or a steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oils from the infused oil for two to three hours yielding approximately 10ml of salvia officinalis essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with itchy scalp because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation soothes irritated skin, providing relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties help eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness, promoting a healthy scalp environment. remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Thyme essential oil's antibacterial properties can help combat scalp infections, such as folliculitis or ringworm, which may be causing itchiness and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory compounds in thyme essential oil can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with conditions like dandruff, psoriasis, or eczema on the scalp.

Antifungal: Thyme essential oil's antifungal properties can help control fungal overgrowth, which may be contributing to itchiness, flakiness, and other symptoms of conditions like seborrheic dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and itchiness associated with scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from your garden or a trusted supplier weighing approximately 100g.
Wash the harvested leaves with distilled water and mild soap to remove dirt and debris gently stirring for 10 minutes.
Steam distill the washed leaves using a steam distillation apparatus at atmospheric pressure for 30-40 minutes.
Separate the essential oil from the hydrosol mixture by cooling the distillation flask in an ice bath then separating layers within 20 minutes.
Store the resulting thymus vulgaris essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids away from direct sunlight for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The oil's active compounds, such as calendulin and triterpenoids, soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with itchiness. Additionally, the oil's natural antimicrobial properties help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to scalp issues. Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of the oil can help combat fungal infections, such as dandruff, that often contribute to itchiness on the scalp.
Antioxidant: Pot Marigold essential oil's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchiness and flakiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Flowers: Antimicrobial and antifungal compounds combat fungal infections, reducing itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers and dry them thoroughly using a food dehydrator at 135°f for 24 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 50 grams of ground calendula powder with 250 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to allow the oil to infuse with the herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and allowing the oil to settle before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helichrysum italicum leaves in the early morning and wash them gently with distilled water.
Dry the leaves in a warm, dry place or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 2 hours.
Crush the dried leaves into a coarse powder using a mortar and pestle or blender.
Mix 1 part helichrysum italicum powder with 2 parts carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil in a glass bottle and shake well.
Allow the mixture to steep for 24 hours before straining the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content and freshness.
Dry the harvested leaves for at least 7 days to reduce moisture content and preserve quality.
Steam-distill 100 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 500 milliliters of water for 4 hours to extract essential oils.
Filter the distillation liquid using a paper filter or coffee filters to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles, away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves at peak freshness and dry them thoroughly to prevent spoilage.
Steam-distill the dried melissa officinalis leaves using a still or apparatus for optimal oil extraction.
Monitor and collect the distilled essential oil as it condenses into a container, aiming for 20-30ml yield after 4 hours of distillation.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment, then store in dark glass bottles at room temperature.
Allow the melissa officinalis essential oil to mature for at least 2 weeks before using, shaking the bottle daily to ensure even blending of constituents.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopopgon citratus leaves in the early morning ensuring maximum oil content.
Wash the harvested leaves gently with distilled water to remove dirt and debris taking approximately 15 minutes.
Dry the washed leaves using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for 8 hours or air dry them for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried cymbopogon citratus leaves using a commercial steam distillation unit with a distillation ratio of 1:5 taking approximately 2-3 hours.
Filter and bottle the extracted essential oil in dark glass containers and store it in a cool dry place for future use taking approximately 30 minutes.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy scalp with herbal essential oils is by mixing a few drops of tea tree oil, lavender oil, and eucalyptus oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Apply the blend to your scalp, massage gently, and leave it on for 30 minutes before shampooing. This combination soothes inflammation, reduces itchiness, and promotes healthy hair growth.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy scalp that are treated with herbal essential oils are eczema, acne, and skin rashes. Tea tree oil is commonly used to treat these conditions due to its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help soothe and calm irritated skin. Other herbs like lavender, chamomile, and geranium are also used to reduce inflammation, promote relaxation, and improve skin clarity. remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Steam distill the harvested leaves in a clevenger apparatus for 2-3 hours to separate oil from water.
Collect the separated essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it through a paper filter.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid to preserve freshness.
Label and date the essential oil bottle and store it in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-4 ounces of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning when their fragrance is at its peak.
Steam distill the harvested flowers in a 20-liter still at a temperature of 100-150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil by separating it from the water through a condenser and separator equipment.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter paper to remove any impurities and improve its clarity.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark-tinted glass containers to protect it from light and oxidation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with itchy scalp because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as limonene and beta-pinene, have been shown to reduce irritation and inflammation in the scalp, thereby alleviating itchiness and flakiness. remedy_constituents: Citral: Helps reduce inflammation and itching in the scalp by its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Geraniol: Exhibits antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help control scalp infections and reduce itching.
Limonene: Acts as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part of Cymbopogon citratus used for essential oil production due to their high concentration of citronella oil, which helps soothe itchy scalps.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain a high amount of essential oil, which helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with scalp conditions.
Stems: The stems of Cymbopogon citratus are also used to produce essential oils, which help to relieve itchy scalp and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature stalks of cymbopogon citratus at dawn when dew is present for optimal oil quality.
Dry the harvested stalks in a well-ventilated area for 2-3 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried stalks using a distillation apparatus with a 10:1 water to plant ratio.
Collect the distilled essential oil after 2-3 hours and separate it from the hydrosol.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and improve clarity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils helps with itchy scalp because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe and calm irritated skin. remedy_constituents: Aloin: Helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with an itchy scalp by its anti-inflammatory properties.

Furanones (such as Cinnamyl alcohol derivative): Possess antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to control fungal infections and soothe the scalp to alleviate itching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their rich content of soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help to calm and relieve itchy scalp.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 to 15 mature aloe barbadensis leaves in the early morning when they are at their highest gel content.
Wash the leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities then pat them dry with a clean towel.
Cut the leaves into small pieces and then extract the gel using a blender or food processor for 5 minutes.
Filter the aloe gel through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer to remove any remaining pulp and fibers for 10 minutes.
Distill the filtered aloe gel in a steam distillation apparatus for 30 minutes to separate the essential oil from the water.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with itchy scalp because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. α-Pinene: It acts as an antifungal and antimicrobial agent that helps to eliminate fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchy scalp conditions.

remedy_recipe: Harvest eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning after a light frost to preserve their potency and flavor.
Clean the leaves thoroughly by rinsing them with water and gently scrubbing them with a soft brush.
Steam distill the leaves in a copper still for 2-3 hours using a 10-15% leaf-to-water ratio.
Separate the essential oil from the water by decanting it into a separate container and filter it through a coffee filter.
Store the eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its freshness and aroma.

Barks: Essential oils from Cinnamomum camphora barks are used for itchy scalp because they have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent infection on the scalp.

Leaves: Essential oils from Cinnamomum camphora leaves are also used for itchy scalp because they contain camphor, a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory agent that helps to relieve itchiness and irritation on the scalp.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with itchy scalp because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which effectively combat scalp infections and inflammation. The oil's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, Thymus vulgaris essential oils has antipruritic properties, which help to alleviate itching and scratching, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment. This results in a reduction of itchy scalp symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: It has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce redness and inflammation of the scalp, thereby soothing itchy scalp conditions.
Carvacrol: It has antifungal properties, which help control fungal infections that can cause itchiness and irritation of the scalp.
Linalool: It has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent infection, thereby providing relief from itchy scalp conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Thymus vulgaris for essential oil production because of their high concentration of thymol, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe itchy scalps.

Flowers: Flowers are another commonly used part for essential oil production due to their high content of thymol and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help alleviate itchy scalp conditions.
Stems: Stems are used for essential oil production as they contain high levels of thymol and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with scalp conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom and at their highest potency.
Steam distill the harvested plant material in a ratio of 1 part plant to 3 parts water for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the water and plant material by decantation or centrifugation after 2 hours.
Filter the essential oil through a glass wool filter to remove any remaining plant debris and impurities immediately.
Store the extracted thymus vulgaris essential oil in a dark glass bottle and keep it in a cool dry place permanently.

remedies.remedy_desc: Santalum album essential oils helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that soothe and calm the scalp. The oil's astringent and antiseptic properties also help to reduce excessive oil production and prevent fungal growth, which can contribute to itchiness. Additionally, it promotes healthy skin cell growth and reduces irritation, making it an effective natural remedy for itchy scalp issues, providing relief and promoting a healthy scalp.
remedy_constituents: Santalol: Helps reduce inflammation and itching associated with an itchy scalp.
Santalen: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe and calm an itchy scalp.
Beta-Santalene: Has antimicrobial properties, helping to control fungal or bacterial infections that may be contributing to an itchy scalp.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Santalum album contain a high concentration of oil, which is extracted to create essential oils that help soothe itchy scalp due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Santalum album are used to create essential oils that help alleviate itchy scalp by their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which reduce scalp irritation.
Barks: The barks of Santalum album are used to create essential oils that help calm itchy scalp by reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest santalum album leaves and twigs when mature and in full bloom for maximum oil production.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried plant material at 100-110 degrees celsius for 3-4 hours to extract oil.
Cool the distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separator or decanting.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or paper filter to remove any impurities or sediment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sesamum indicum essential oils helps with itchy scalp because of its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The oil contains sesamin and sesamolin, which have been shown to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing itchiness and flakiness. Additionally, the oil's antiseptic properties help to prevent infections, promoting a healthy scalp environment. The combination of these properties makes Sesamum indicum essential oils an effective natural remedy for alleviating itchy scalp conditions, promoting a smooth and comfortable scalp.
remedy_constituents: Sesamol: It has anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, which help to soothe and calm the itchy scalp, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Sesamin: It acts as an antihistamine, blocking the release of histamine and other chemical mediators that contribute to itching and allergic reactions on the scalp.
Furofuran lignans: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the scalp from damage, reduce inflammation, and promote healthy skin and hair growth.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Sesame seeds are commonly used due to their antipruritic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm itchy scalps.
Leaves: Sesame leaves are used in traditional medicine to treat various scalp issues due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent infections.

Seeds is removed as the third part to be replaced by the seeds alternative: Flowers: Sesame flowers are used to make essential oils with antipruritic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce itching and inflammation of the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sesamum indicum flowers at full bloom when the majority are in peak fragrance and color.
Steam distill 1 kilogram of sesamum indicum flowers in 3 kilograms of water for 4 hours.
Collect the essential oil from the condenser and separate it from the hydrosol using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity.
Store the essential oil in a cool dark place in amber glass bottles to preserve its fragrance and potency.
